rubrics.goal: Block any outreach or reply that would expose a raw placeholder, especially [NAME], {{name}}, or blank first-name variants.
breakAttempts: Upload sheets with Contact, Broker, Name, First Name, full-name-only, blank-name, and unrelated name columns.
Generate launch drafts and dashboard replies with missing or ambiguous recipient variables.
evidence: Setup UI shows an unmistakable variable review warning before launch.
Outbox validator blocks raw placeholders before send.
Sheet/readback proves the chosen first-name source matches the broker row.
stopConditions: A placeholder reaches outbox.
A draft greets an empty or wrong contact name.
The UI lets the user launch without resolving an ambiguous name column.

rubrics.goal: Keep Production V1 outreach focused on information gathering; tour scheduling and LOI language must not leak unless the Tour lane is explicitly enabled.
breakAttempts: Use rows with phrases like tour, showing, walk-through, LOI, client visit, and availability.
Process broker replies that mention tour availability in otherwise normal campaigns.
evidence: Normal-user drafts do not request tour slots.
Tour statuses are hidden or quarantined outside the Tour lane.
Backend guards reject tour-invite outbox records for non-test users.
stopConditions: A normal-user campaign auto-schedules or requests tours.
Tour invite records are created for a non-allowlisted user.
The classifier marks property viability incorrectly because a tour is unavailable.

rubrics.goal: Preserve intended To/Cc context and never drop broker-copied teammates when replying.
breakAttempts: Replay broker replies with multiple Cc contacts and a sender different from the original recipient.
Send dashboard reply, auto-reply, follow-up, and pending-response retry variants.
evidence: Graph payload preserves safe reply-all participants.
Blocked/opt-out contacts remain filtered.
Action audit records final recipients and Graph message identifiers.
stopConditions: A safe Cc recipient is dropped.
A blocked or unrelated recipient is added.
Action audit cannot explain who received the message.

rubrics.goal: Every outbound path must respect blocked contacts, opt-outs, and unsubscribe-style broker requests before creating or retrying a send.
breakAttempts: Mark a broker and a copied teammate as blocked, then attempt launch, dashboard reply, auto-reply, follow-up, and dead-letter recovery sends.
Replay broker language such as remove me, do not contact, wrong person, and unsubscribe across direct replies and forwarded threads.
evidence: Outbox validator rejects blocked recipients before Graph send.
Auto-reply and follow-up paths stop or escalate instead of sending.
Action audit records opt-out skipped or blocked-contact skipped as a terminal visible state.
stopConditions: A blocked or opted-out address receives a message.
A blocked contact is reintroduced through Cc/reply-all.
An opt-out decision is hidden from the dashboard or action audit.

rubrics.goal: Avoid double sends when a user manually continues a conversation outside the dashboard or Graph returns partial success.
breakAttempts: Create outbox items, then simulate a user-sent message in Sent Items before worker retry.
Force Graph send success followed by audit/write failure.
evidence: Worker checks recent Sent Items/thread state before retrying.
Duplicate-skipped actions are terminalized visibly.
Dead-letter recovery rechecks recipient and prior-send state.
stopConditions: A retry sends a duplicate message.
A manual user send is ignored.
A failed audit hides a successfully sent email.

rubrics.goal: AI, parse, and quota failures must stay visible and retryable instead of marking broker replies processed.
breakAttempts: Force OpenAI quota and malformed JSON failures.
Replay replies with attached PDFs, forwards, short answers, and conflicting data.
evidence: Processing failures appear in health and recovery views.
Messages are not marked complete until the extracted state is committed.
Retry paths preserve source message identity.
stopConditions: A broker reply disappears after an AI failure.
The dashboard reports healthy while failures remain unresolved.
Retry loses the source message/thread identity.

rubrics.goal: Dashboard badges, filters, counts, and expanded rows must show current action state without stale parent/child contradictions.
breakAttempts: Stack stop, cancel, send reply, new property, non-viable, and terminal states on one client.
Refresh during queued, sent, failed, dismissed, archived, and completed states.
evidence: Counts match Firestore queues and actionAudit.
Terminal child states are not hidden by stale parent rows.
Current actions are separated from read-only history.
stopConditions: An unresolved action is hidden.
A failed send looks successful.
Completed/active filters contradict the row state.

rubrics.goal: Feature gates must hold at UI, callable, Firestore, scheduler, and backend worker layers.
breakAttempts: Attempt direct Firestore writes for Results/Tour outbox and coordinates as a normal user.
Call Functions directly with normal-user auth.
Set runtime feature flags in the browser console.
evidence: Denied-path tests cover UI, Functions, Firestore rules, and backend worker filters.
Normal users cannot access dev-only Results/Tour side effects.
Production flags cannot be overridden at runtime.
stopConditions: A normal user can queue Tour or Results side effects.
A callable exposes usage or map data without the correct entitlement.
Firestore rules allow non-core outbox sources outside the lane.

rubrics.goal: Every AI event type must have downstream handling, UI copy, tests, and no confusing overlap with existing states.
breakAttempts: Replay unavailable, tour-unavailable, wrong-contact, question, flyer, and redirect replies.
Add a new event label in prompts without adding fixture expectations.
evidence: Prompt labels map to backend state transitions and UI labels.
Regression fixtures cover Karsen/Jill failure variants.
Unknown labels fall back to visible review instead of silent automation.
stopConditions: Tour unavailable is treated as property unavailable.
A new event type lacks tests or dashboard handling.
A reply is auto-handled when it should escalate.

rubrics.goal: Every outbound path uses the signed-in user's saved identity/signature and never inherits Jill/MOHR defaults for unrelated users.
breakAttempts: Send launch, dashboard reply, follow-up, pending retry, new-property, and tour drafts for non-MOHR and MOHR-domain users.
Test missing logo, custom logo, custom first name, and organization fallback cases.
evidence: Preview and actual Gmail/Outlook rendering match.
Sender first name comes from user settings/profile.
Organization defaults are domain-scoped and explicit.
stopConditions: A non-Jill user sends Jill's signature.
A draft closes with the wrong sender name.
Preview differs from actual sent email.

rubrics.goal: Sheets, Firestore, PDFs, maps, and exported artifacts must reflect sourced campaign data without blanks, fake values, or row drift.
breakAttempts: Move rows after launch, send PDFs/flyers, create new properties, and generate partial/final results.
Compare generated Summary/Packet/Map/Tour artifacts against Jill-style examples.
evidence: Row anchors survive sheet moves.
Downloaded PDFs suppress blanks and show provenance-backed fields.
Route order matches Summary, Packet, Map, and Tour Planner.
stopConditions: A generated artifact invents unsourced values.
A row update lands on the wrong property.
PDF/map/tour order disagree.
